meta.feed_title: Al Jazeera – Breaking News, World News and Video from Al Jazeera

items.title: Spain’s former PM Zapatero faces corruption probe
items.summary: High court is investigating Zapatero over airline aid, putting pressure on current PM.

items.title: WHO to hold emergency committee meeting as Ebola death toll rises to 131
items.summary: A panel of experts ⁠led by ⁠the WHO is to meet to discuss vaccine ⁠options for new epidemic.

items.title: Iran squad in race against time for World Cup readiness amid US-Israel war
items.summary: With Iran's local football league suspended since March, most players in the squad have not been in action for months.

items.title: The US, Israel and the normalisation of scandal
items.summary: Thanks to Netanyahu and Trump, scandal is now our new normal - call it 'Everythinggate'.

items.title: Khartoum’s slow recovery amid cautious return
items.summary: War-damaged Khartoum sees uneven recovery as residents weigh return amid fragile services and economy.

items.title: Starbucks Korea CEO fired over promotion that evoked military crackdown
items.summary: 'Tank Day' marketing campaign provoked backlash for eliciting bloody suppression of 1980 Gwangju uprising.

items.title: ‘China holds the cards’: Why Putin’s visit to Beijing after Trump matters
items.summary: Scant progress in US-China talks bolsters Putin, while for China, hosting back-to-back visits is a diplomatic flex.

items.title: Child survivor of San Diego mosque shooting describes ordeal
items.summary: A boy who survived the San Diego mosque shooting that killed three people has described seeing ‘bad stuff’.

items.title: At least 10 dead as huge floods sweep southern and central China
items.summary: The affected area extends more than 1,000km (620 miles), according to Chinese meteorologists.

items.title: ‘King of the North’: Who is Andy Burnham, a potential UK prime minister?
items.summary: With Keir Starmer under fire, it is Manchester’s mayor who looks to be the key challenger.

items.title: San Diego mosque shooting: What we know about the victims and the attackers
items.summary: Authorities said the attack at the Islamic Center of San Diego is being investigated as a possible hate crime.

items.title: Gunmen abduct 39 students, 7 teachers in attacks on Nigeria schools
items.summary: The attacks targeted a secondary school and two primary schools, as gunmen kidnapped children aged between two and 16.

items.title: Lebanon and Syria reshape ties amid Israeli attacks and regional shifts
items.summary: Damascus and Beirut focus on sovereignty and cooperation, moving away from decades of Syrian dominance over Lebanon.

items.title: Los Angeles World Cup stadium workers threaten strike over ICE deployment
items.summary: Workers represented by a local union say ICE presence would create a climate of fear during the FIFA World Cup 2026.

items.title: Palestine weekly wrap: Jerusalem Day, Nakba mark week of surging attacks
items.summary: Far-right marchers in the Old City chant "death to Arabs" while Israel kills Hamas military chief in Gaza.

items.title: Russian strike damages Ukraine Danube port as Moscow intercepts drones
items.summary: Russia also plans military exercises as Putin prepares for Beijing visit.

items.title: Iran war day 81: Trump delays attack; Tehran says won’t ‘surrender’
items.summary: US president says he held off a scheduled attack on Iran after pressure from Gulf allies as renewed talks gain momentum.

items.title: India-Nordic summit: Why is Modi wooing Northern Europe?
items.summary: Technology, green transition, geopolitics and India's role in the Arctic region are among key topics on the agenda.

items.title: Wembanyama scores 41 as Spurs upset Thunder in Game 1 thriller
items.summary: French centre dominates as San Antonio steal a crucial 122–115 road win against defending NBA champions.

items.title: Bolivian miners clash with police demanding president resign
items.summary: Bolivian miners clash with police demanding that President Paz resign.

items.title: Helicopters douse flames threatening hillside homes in California
items.summary: Video shows helicopters dumping water on a fast-moving wildfire in southern California’s Simi Valley.

items.title: How Philadelphia’s Democratic primary tests the bounds of US progressivism
items.summary: The race for Pennsylvania's third congressional district reveals divisions between Democrats closely aligned on policy.

items.title: Trump’s repeated ultimatums betray his lack of leverage over Iran
items.summary: Foad Izadi says US President Trump's repeated threats of renewed full-scale war are a sign of weakness.

items.title: WHO responding to ‘complex, difficult’ Ebola outbreak in DR Congo
items.summary: The World Health Organization is getting health experts and supplies to DR Congo to battle the Ebola outbreak.

items.title: Russia-China ties ‘stabilising’ force in world, Putin says before Xi talks
items.summary: Russia's Vladimir Putin is travelling to China for second meeting with Chinese leader Xi Jinping in less than a year.
